specification.topic-statement: The token estimator converts a byte count into an estimated number of tokens using a fixed bytes-per-token ratio.

scope.in-scope: Converting a byte count to an estimated token count by dividing by a fixed ratio.

behavior.estimation:
The byte count is divided by 4 using integer division (remainders are discarded). The quotient is returned as a signed 32-bit integer.
A byte count of 0, 1, 2, or 3 each produce an estimate of 0. Every group of 4 bytes produces an estimate of 1 additional token.
The result is not range-checked before being stored as a signed 32-bit integer. When the byte count divided by 4 exceeds 2,147,483,647 (roughly 8.6 billion bytes), the behavior is undefined by the C11 standard. In practice this produces a silently incorrect (typically negative) result with no error indication.
